desc: Ohrid layers a UNESCO-listed Orthodox old town over a deep tectonic lake, with the year defined by spring church-and-viewpoint walks, high-summer swimming and boat life, then quieter cultural weekends in autumn.
hls: UNESCO church skyline above one of Europe's oldest lakes
Strong summer identity: beaches, boat trips, and lakeside promenades
Ottoman-era bazaar lanes and Macedonian-Albanian borderland food culture
Clear shoulder-season value when weather stays pleasant but crowds drop

todo.name: Kaneo-Plaošnik church ridge walk
todo.desc: Weather-dependent: best in dry conditions for lake panoramas and sunset viewpoints.

todo.name: Lake Ohrid boat run to Sveti Naum
todo.desc: Weather-dependent: calm-water days are best for longer crossings and swim stops.

todo.name: Bay of Bones Museum
todo.desc: All-weather option with indoor exhibits and reconstructed stilt-settlement sections.

todo.name: National Workshop for Handmade Paper
todo.desc: All-weather stop near old-town churches with demos of traditional paper techniques.

todo.name: Samuel's Fortress walls
todo.desc: Go early or late for cooler temperatures and clearer photos in summer.

todo.name: Čaršija tavern dinner crawl
todo.desc: Sample Ohrid trout and tavče gravče in old-bazaar lanes after lakefront walks.

wifi.note: Generally solid in old-town hotels; speed dips at full summer occupancy.

prac.fltNote: OHD is the closest arrival point in peak season, but many UK/Ireland itineraries route via Skopje (SKP) or Tirana (TIA) because frequency is higher; onward travel is typically 15-20 min by taxi from OHD, ~3-4 hr by coach/car from SKP, or ~2.5-3 hr from TIA depending on border traffic.

airports.name: Ohrid St. Paul the Apostle Airport

prac.lang: Macedonian is the primary language; Albanian is also widely spoken, and English is common in tourism services.
prac.tz: CET (UTC+1) in winter, CEST (UTC+2) in summer.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Lakefront roads and parking are congested in July-August evenings.
Some beach clubs and boat operators are cash-only despite card terminals in town restaurants.
Winter service frequency drops sharply for intercity buses—check last departures in advance.

sweet: May-June and September usually balance warm lake days with easier hotel pricing; July-August has the best swim weather but the highest crowding and transfer bottlenecks.
